ARTICLE_TITLE: Son Heung-min Leads Tottenham to Champions League Final with Hat-trick Against Real Madrid
ARTICLE_BODY: Son Heung-min delivered a spectacular performance in Tottenham's 4-1 victory over Real Madrid in the Champions League semi-final second leg at Wembley Stadium. The Korean captain's hat-trick secured Spurs' first Champions League final appearance since 2019. Son opened the scoring in the 23rd minute with a brilliant solo effort, cutting inside from the left wing before curling the ball into the top corner. His second goal came from a well-timed header following Harry Kane's cross, while his third was a clinical finish after a counter-attack. The 34-year-old forward's exceptional form this season has been instrumental in Tottenham's European campaign. Manager Ange Postecoglou praised Son's leadership qualities and described the performance as 'world-class.' Korean fans celebrated nationwide as Son became the first Asian player to score a Champions League semi-final hat-trick, further cementing his legacy in football history.
